title: helianthus rigidus var. subrhomboideus
intro: Helianthus rigidus var. subrhomboideus, also known as the stiff sunflower, is a variety of perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Helianthus rigidus var. subrhomboideus include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or tincture that can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
